items.summary: Implement an MVP-only Google-style identity login flow that results in a backend session for the caller and stores only identity fields (email, name, avatar) without assigning any role during login.

Add a backend User model (separate from Player/Team profile models) with fields `id`, `email`, `name`, `avatar`, and `userType` where `userType` is nullable (null | player | team), and store it per authenticated caller.
Implement backend APIs `getCurrentUser()` and `setUserType(userType)` where `setUserType` can only be called once per user and fails if `userType` is already set.

Enforce role-based access control in the backend so that player-only APIs reject team users and team-only APIs reject player users, using the stored immutable `userType` as the source of truth (not client-provided parameters).

Update the frontend login page to use a Google-style login button and, after login, route users based on backend `getCurrentUser()` as follows: if `userType` is null redirect to `/select-role`; if player go to Player onboarding; if team go to Team onboarding.

Create/adjust a dedicated role-selection route `/select-role` that is mandatory when `userType` is null and includes exactly two role choices (Player / Team) and a clear warning that the choice is permanent; selecting a role calls `setUserType` and then routes to the appropriate onboarding flow.

Add frontend routing guards so that authenticated users with `userType` unset (null) are always redirected to `/select-role`, and users with `userType` set are prevented from accessing `/select-role` (redirect to their correct onboarding/dashboard).
